steps.title: Prepare the Base
steps.instruction: Blend 4 tomatoes, 1 onion, 5 cashews, and 2 cloves of garlic into a smooth paste.
steps.why: The cashews add creaminess and body to the gravy without using excess cream.
steps.tip: Use ripe red tomatoes for a naturally sweet and rich base.

steps.title: Cook the Gravy
steps.instruction: Heat 2 tbsp butter in a pan. Add the blended paste and cook on medium heat for 10-12 minutes until oil separates.
steps.why: Cooking until oil separates ensures the raw taste of tomatoes is gone and flavors are concentrated.
steps.tip: Stir frequently to prevent burning at the bottom.

steps.title: Add Spices
steps.instruction: Add 1 tsp garam masala, 1/2 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chili powder, and salt to taste. Cook for 2 minutes.
steps.why: Blooming spices in fat releases their essential oils and deepens flavor.
steps.tip: Reduce chili powder if you prefer a milder taste.

steps.title: Add Cream & Paneer
steps.instruction: Add 1/4 cup cream and cubed paneer. Simmer for 5-7 minutes on low heat.
steps.why: Low heat prevents paneer from becoming rubbery and allows it to absorb flavors.
steps.tip: Soak paneer cubes in warm water for 10 mins before adding for extra softness.

steps.title: Finish & Garnish
steps.instruction: Add 1 tsp kasuri methi (dried fenugreek leaves), a knob of butter, and garnish with cream.
steps.why: Kasuri methi adds an earthy, aromatic finish that defines this dish.
steps.tip: Crush kasuri methi between your palms before adding to release maximum aroma.

steps.title: Soak Rice
steps.instruction: Wash and soak 2 cups basmati rice in water for 30 minutes. Parboil with whole spices until 70% cooked.
steps.why: Soaking ensures even cooking and prevents breakage. Parboiling means the rice finishes cooking in the dum.
steps.tip: Add a few drops of lemon juice to keep rice grains separate and white.

steps.title: Prepare Vegetables
steps.instruction: Chop carrots, beans, peas, potatoes, and cauliflower. Fry them lightly in ghee for 5 minutes.
steps.why: Light frying seals the vegetables and prevents them from becoming mushy during the dum process.
steps.tip: Cut all vegetables to similar sizes for even cooking.

steps.title: Make Masala Base
steps.instruction: Fry sliced onions until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste, yogurt, biryani masala, and cook for 8 minutes.
steps.why: Golden onions provide the signature sweetness and color to biryani.
steps.tip: Patience with onions is key — rush this step and the biryani will lack depth.

steps.title: Layer & Dum Cook
steps.instruction: Layer vegetables and rice alternately. Top with saffron milk, mint, coriander, and ghee. Seal and cook on low for 20 minutes.
steps.why: Dum cooking (sealed pot) traps steam, allowing flavors to meld and rice to absorb aromatics.
steps.tip: Use dough to seal the lid for an authentic dum. No peeking!

steps.title: Rest & Serve
steps.instruction: Let biryani rest for 5 minutes. Gently mix layers and serve with raita.
steps.why: Resting allows steam to redistribute, making every grain fluffy and flavorful.
steps.tip: Use a flat spatula to mix gently without breaking rice grains.

steps.title: Melt Chocolate & Butter
steps.instruction: Melt 200g dark chocolate with 100g butter in a double boiler until smooth.
steps.why: A double boiler provides gentle, even heat preventing chocolate from seizing or burning.
steps.tip: Use chocolate with at least 70% cocoa for a rich, intense flavor.

steps.title: Mix Wet Ingredients
steps.instruction: Whisk 2 eggs with 2 egg yolks and 1/4 cup sugar until thick and pale (about 5 minutes).
steps.why: Whisking incorporates air, creating the cake's light texture around the molten center.
steps.tip: Room temperature eggs whip up better than cold ones.

steps.title: Combine
steps.instruction: Fold the chocolate mixture into the egg mixture. Sift in 2 tbsp flour and a pinch of salt. Fold gently.
steps.why: Gentle folding keeps the air in the batter — over-mixing makes the cake dense instead of light.
steps.tip: Fold until just combined; a few streaks are okay.

steps.title: Bake
steps.instruction: Pour into greased ramekins. Bake at 220°C (425°F) for exactly 12-14 minutes.
steps.why: High heat cooks the outside while keeping the center molten — timing is critical here.
steps.tip: The cake is done when edges are firm but center jiggles slightly.

steps.title: Serve Immediately
steps.instruction: Let cool for 1 minute, run a knife around edges, invert onto a plate. Dust with powdered sugar.
steps.why: The molten center will solidify if left too long, so speed is essential.
steps.tip: Serve with a scoop of vanilla ice cream for the perfect contrast!

steps.title: Marinate Chicken
steps.instruction: Marinate 500g chicken with yogurt, turmeric, chili powder, and salt for at least 30 minutes.
steps.why: Yogurt tenderizes the meat while spices penetrate deep for maximum flavor.
steps.tip: Marinate overnight in the fridge for the best results.

steps.title: Grill Chicken
steps.instruction: Grill or pan-fry marinated chicken pieces until charred and cooked through.
steps.why: Charring adds a smoky, tandoori-like flavor that defines butter chicken.
steps.tip: Don't move the chicken too often — let it develop a proper sear.

steps.title: Make the Gravy
steps.instruction: Blend tomatoes, cashews, and onion. Cook in butter for 15 minutes until thick. Add cream and spices.
steps.why: The long cooking time removes the raw tomato acidity and creates a velvety, rich gravy.
steps.tip: Strain the gravy through a sieve for a restaurant-quality smooth texture.

steps.title: Combine
steps.instruction: Add grilled chicken to the gravy. Simmer on low for 10 minutes.
steps.why: Simmering allows the chicken to absorb the gravy flavors and become more tender.
steps.tip: Add a splash of water if the gravy gets too thick.

steps.title: Finish
steps.instruction: Add kasuri methi, a swirl of cream, and a knob of butter. Serve with naan or rice.
steps.why: The final butter adds richness while kasuri methi provides the signature aroma.
steps.tip: A squeeze of lemon juice at the end brightens all the flavors beautifully.

steps.title: Make Croutons
steps.instruction: Cut bread into cubes, toss with olive oil, garlic, salt. Bake at 190°C for 10 minutes until golden.
steps.why: Homemade croutons are crunchier and more flavorful than store-bought.
steps.tip: Toss them halfway through baking for even browning.

steps.title: Prepare Dressing
steps.instruction: Whisk together egg yolk, lemon juice, minced garlic, mustard, worcestershire sauce. Slowly drizzle in olive oil while whisking.
steps.why: Slowly adding oil creates an emulsion — the creamy, thick texture of Caesar dressing.
steps.tip: Use a pasteurized egg if concerned about raw eggs.

steps.title: Assemble
steps.instruction: Tear romaine lettuce, toss with dressing, top with croutons and shaved parmesan.
steps.why: Tearing lettuce creates more surface area for dressing to cling to than cutting.
steps.tip: Chill the lettuce in ice water for 10 mins before serving for extra crunch.

steps.title: Prepare Salmon
steps.instruction: Pat salmon dry. Season with salt, pepper, minced garlic, and drizzle with olive oil and lemon juice.
steps.why: Patting dry ensures a crispy skin. Moisture is the enemy of a good sear.
steps.tip: Let salmon sit at room temperature for 15 minutes before cooking.

steps.title: Grill
steps.instruction: Place salmon skin-side down on a hot grill. Cook for 4-5 minutes per side.
steps.why: Starting skin-side down protects the flesh and makes the skin crispy.
steps.tip: Don't move the fish until it releases naturally from the grill.

steps.title: Serve
steps.instruction: Top with fresh dill, a squeeze of lemon, and a pat of butter. Serve with steamed vegetables.
steps.why: Butter on hot fish melts into a simple, luxurious sauce.
steps.tip: The salmon is done when it flakes easily with a fork but is still slightly translucent in the center.

steps.title: Blend
steps.instruction: Blend 1 ripe mango, 1 cup yogurt, 1/2 cup milk, 2 tbsp sugar, and a pinch of cardamom until smooth.
steps.why: Using ripe mango ensures natural sweetness — you may need less sugar.
steps.tip: Use frozen mango chunks for a thicker, colder lassi without adding ice.

steps.title: Adjust & Serve
steps.instruction: Taste and adjust sweetness. Pour into glasses and garnish with a sprinkle of cardamom or saffron strands.
steps.why: Adjusting to taste is important since mango sweetness varies by variety and ripeness.
steps.tip: For a richer lassi, use Greek yogurt instead of regular yogurt.

steps.title: Marinate Chicken
steps.instruction: Marinate chicken pieces with yogurt, biryani masala, ginger-garlic paste, salt, and lemon juice for 1 hour.
steps.why: Long marination breaks down proteins for tender, flavorful meat that absorbs spices deeply.
steps.tip: Score the chicken pieces for better marinade penetration.

steps.title: Prepare Rice
steps.instruction: Soak basmati rice for 30 minutes. Parboil with whole spices until 70% cooked. Drain.
steps.why: Parboiling ensures the rice finishes perfectly during dum without becoming mushy.
steps.tip: The rice should still have a bite in the center — it will finish cooking in steam.

steps.title: Cook Chicken
steps.instruction: Fry sliced onions until deep golden. Add marinated chicken and cook for 15 minutes until half done.
steps.why: Half-cooking the chicken prevents it from drying out during the long dum process.
steps.tip: The onions should be almost caramelized — this is the soul of biryani.

steps.title: Layer & Dum
steps.instruction: Layer chicken and rice. Top with saffron milk, mint, fried onions, and ghee. Seal pot tightly, cook on lowest heat for 25 minutes.
steps.why: The sealed pot creates a steam chamber where flavors from chicken and rice marry together.
steps.tip: Place a tawa (flat pan) under the pot to prevent the bottom from burning.

steps.title: Rest & Serve
steps.instruction: Don't open for 5 minutes after turning off heat. Gently mix and serve with raita and salan.
steps.why: Resting completes the cooking process and allows the rice to firm up perfectly.
steps.tip: Serve from the pot at the table for a dramatic presentation!

steps.title: Sauté Mushrooms
steps.instruction: Slice mushrooms and sauté in olive oil and butter until golden brown. Season with salt and thyme. Set aside.
steps.why: Cooking mushrooms separately ensures they get properly browned instead of steaming in the risotto.
steps.tip: Don't stir mushrooms too much — let them develop a golden crust.

steps.title: Toast Rice
steps.instruction: In the same pan, sauté diced onion and garlic. Add arborio rice and toast for 2 minutes until edges are translucent.
steps.why: Toasting the rice creates a nutty flavor and helps it absorb liquid gradually for the perfect creamy texture.
steps.tip: You'll hear the rice start to click against the pan — that's when it's toasted.

steps.title: Add Wine & Broth
steps.instruction: Add white wine and stir until absorbed. Then add warm broth one ladle at a time, stirring frequently.
steps.why: Gradual addition of warm broth is the key to risotto — it releases starch slowly, creating creaminess.
steps.tip: Keep the broth warm in a separate pot. Cold broth shocks the rice and slows cooking.

steps.title: Finish
steps.instruction: When rice is al dente (about 18-20 minutes), stir in mushrooms, parmesan, and a knob of butter.
steps.why: This final step, called 'mantecatura', creates the signature creamy, wave-like consistency.
steps.tip: The risotto should flow like lava when you shake the pan — add more broth if too thick.

steps.title: Bloom Curry Paste
steps.instruction: Heat thick coconut cream (top of the can) in a wok. Add 3 tbsp green curry paste and fry for 3 minutes.
steps.why: Frying the paste in coconut cream blooms the spices and deepens the curry's flavor complexity.
steps.tip: Look for the oil separating from the cream — that's when the paste is properly bloomed.

steps.title: Cook Chicken
steps.instruction: Add sliced chicken and cook for 5 minutes until sealed on all sides.
steps.why: Sealing the chicken in the paste coats it with flavor from the start.
steps.tip: Slice chicken against the grain for more tender pieces.

steps.title: Add Coconut Milk & Vegetables
steps.instruction: Pour in remaining coconut milk. Add eggplant, bamboo shoots, and bell pepper. Simmer for 15 minutes.
steps.why: Simmering allows vegetables to cook while the coconut milk creates a rich, aromatic broth.
steps.tip: Add harder vegetables first, softer ones later to avoid overcooking.

steps.title: Season & Serve
steps.instruction: Add fish sauce, sugar, lime juice, and fresh Thai basil. Serve with jasmine rice.
steps.why: Thai cuisine balances sweet, salty, sour, and spicy — these final additions achieve that harmony.
steps.tip: Add basil leaves at the very end and turn off heat — they wilt perfectly in residual heat.

steps.title: Blanch Spinach
steps.instruction: Boil spinach for 2 minutes, then immediately transfer to ice water. Blend into a smooth puree.
steps.why: Blanching and shocking preserves the vibrant green color and removes bitterness.
steps.tip: Don't over-boil — 2 minutes is plenty. Longer cooking turns spinach olive-drab.

steps.title: Prepare Base
steps.instruction: Sauté cumin seeds, then add chopped onion, ginger, garlic, and green chili. Cook until golden.
steps.why: This aromatic base provides depth and complexity to the spinach gravy.
steps.tip: Cumin should sizzle and turn fragrant — about 30 seconds in hot oil.

steps.title: Combine
steps.instruction: Add spinach puree, turmeric, garam masala, and salt. Simmer for 10 minutes.
steps.why: Simmering melds the spices into the spinach and concentrates the flavors.
steps.tip: Add a pinch of sugar to balance the slight bitterness of spinach.

steps.title: Add Paneer & Finish
steps.instruction: Add cubed paneer and cream. Cook for 5 minutes on low heat. Serve with roti or naan.
steps.why: Low heat prevents paneer from turning rubbery while absorbing the spinach flavor.
steps.tip: For extra flavor, lightly fry paneer cubes before adding to the gravy.

steps.title: Season Fish
steps.instruction: Season fish fillets with chili powder, cumin, garlic powder, salt, and lime juice. Let sit for 10 minutes.
steps.why: The acid in lime juice begins to denature the fish proteins, adding tenderness and flavor.
steps.tip: Use a firm white fish like cod or mahi-mahi that won't fall apart.

steps.title: Cook Fish
steps.instruction: Pan-fry or grill fish for 3-4 minutes per side until flaky.
steps.why: High heat creates a crispy exterior while keeping the interior moist.
steps.tip: Don't flip more than once — handle the fish gently.

steps.title: Make Slaw & Sauce
steps.instruction: Mix shredded cabbage with lime juice and salt. Blend yogurt, garlic, lime, and coriander for the sauce.
steps.why: The crunchy, tangy slaw provides texture contrast to the tender fish.
steps.tip: Massage the cabbage with salt to soften it slightly.

steps.title: Assemble
steps.instruction: Warm tortillas, add fish, top with slaw, avocado slices, drizzle of sauce, and fresh coriander.
steps.why: Layering creates perfect bites where every element is present.
steps.tip: Char tortillas directly on a gas flame for authentic flavor and aroma.

steps.title: Press & Cube Tofu
steps.instruction: Press firm tofu 15 mins to remove moisture. Cut into 1-inch cubes and coat in cornstarch.
steps.why: Removing moisture lets tofu get crispy when fried.
steps.tip: Use a heavy pan on paper towels to press efficiently.

steps.title: Fry Tofu Golden
steps.instruction: Heat oil in wok. Fry tofu cubes until golden and crispy on all sides (5-7 mins). Set aside.
steps.why: Crispy tofu holds texture when tossed in sauce.
steps.tip: Don't crowd the wok — fry in batches.

steps.title: Mix Sauce
steps.instruction: Combine soy sauce, rice vinegar, sugar, cornstarch, and sesame oil in a bowl.
steps.why: Pre-mixing sauce prevents cornstarch clumping.
steps.tip: Taste and adjust sweet-sour balance to your preference.

steps.title: Stir-Fry & Toss
steps.instruction: Stir-fry dried chili, garlic, ginger until fragrant. Add bell pepper, scallions, tofu. Pour sauce over and toss. Top with peanuts.
steps.why: High heat creates authentic wok hei flavor.
steps.tip: Work fast — everything cooks quickly in a hot wok!

steps.title: Use Cold Rice
steps.instruction: Use day-old refrigerated rice. Break up any clumps with your hands before cooking.
steps.why: Cold dry rice fries better — fresh rice is too moist and becomes sticky.
steps.tip: Cook extra rice the night before specifically for fried rice.

steps.title: Cook Chicken & Eggs
steps.instruction: Stir-fry diced chicken until cooked. Push aside, scramble eggs in the same wok until just set.
steps.why: Cooking separately ensures properly textured chicken and fluffy eggs.
steps.tip: Eggs should be slightly underdone — they'll finish with the rice.

steps.title: Stir-Fry Everything
steps.instruction: Add garlic, carrots, peas, and rice. Toss continuously on high heat for 3-4 minutes.
steps.why: Constant tossing ensures even heat and prevents burning.
steps.tip: A carbon steel wok gets much hotter — ideal for fried rice.

steps.title: Season & Serve
steps.instruction: Add soy sauce, sesame oil, white pepper. Toss with chicken, eggs, and scallions. Serve immediately.
steps.why: Adding soy sauce last prevents burning and preserves its complex flavor.
steps.tip: A little sesame oil goes a long way — use it as a finishing oil only.

steps.title: Prep Each Vegetable
steps.instruction: Sauté each vegetable separately: spinach with garlic & sesame oil; julienned carrots; mushrooms in soy sauce; zucchini sliced thin.
steps.why: Cooking each vegetable separately keeps distinct textures and flavors.
steps.tip: Blanch spinach 1 min, shock in cold water, then squeeze out excess moisture.

steps.title: Make Gochujang Sauce
steps.instruction: Mix 2 tbsp gochujang, 1 tbsp sesame oil, 1 tbsp sugar, 1 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tsp garlic, 1 tbsp water.
steps.why: The sauce balances sweet, spicy, and savory — the heart of bibimbap.
steps.tip: Adjust gochujang quantity based on your spice preference.

steps.title: Fry Egg Sunny-Side
steps.instruction: Fry an egg in sesame oil keeping the yolk runny.
steps.why: The runny yolk acts as a natural sauce binding all ingredients when mixed.
steps.tip: Cover the pan briefly to set whites while keeping yolk runny.

steps.title: Assemble & Mix
steps.instruction: Place rice in bowl. Arrange vegetables in sections. Top with egg and gochujang sauce. Mix everything together before eating!
steps.why: Mixing is fundamental — the name literally means 'mixed rice'.
steps.tip: Serve in a hot stone bowl for crispy rice bottom — the best part!

steps.title: Double-Coat Chicken
steps.instruction: Mix flour, cornstarch, baking powder, salt. Dip chicken in dry mix, briefly in water, then dry mix again.
steps.why: Double-coating creates the ultra-thin, extra-crispy Korean fried chicken crust.
steps.tip: Baking powder creates air bubbles for extra lightness.

steps.title: Double Fry
steps.instruction: First fry at 160°C for 8-10 mins until pale. Rest 5 mins, then fry again at 180°C for 3-4 mins until golden.
steps.why: Double frying — the Korean secret — first cooks it through, second creates exceptional crunch.
steps.tip: Don't skip the resting period — steam evaporates making it crispier.

steps.title: Make Glaze
steps.instruction: Simmer gochujang, soy sauce, honey, garlic, and ginger in a pan for 3 minutes until slightly thickened.
steps.why: The sauce should be thick enough to coat without making chicken soggy.
steps.tip: Sauce can be made ahead and stored refrigerated for up to a week.

steps.title: Coat & Garnish
steps.instruction: Toss fried chicken in sauce immediately. Garnish with sesame seeds and scallions. Serve right away.
steps.why: Coating immediately keeps the exterior as crispy as possible.
steps.tip: Serve with pickled daikon — a classic Korean pairing that cuts the richness.

steps.title: Make Tare Sauce
steps.instruction: Mix miso paste, soy sauce, sesame oil, and mirin. This concentrated base seasons each bowl.
steps.why: A good tare is the backbone of ramen — it allows personalized seasoning.
steps.tip: Extra tare keeps refrigerated for a month and elevates any soup.

steps.title: Build Broth
steps.instruction: Sauté garlic and ginger. Add vegetable broth, simmer 15 mins. Stir in 2 tbsp tare per serving.
steps.why: Simmering integrates the tare flavors into the broth.
steps.tip: Don't boil after adding miso — high heat destroys its probiotics and delicate flavor.

steps.title: Soft-Boil Eggs
steps.instruction: Boil eggs exactly 6.5 minutes. Ice bath immediately. Peel and marinate in soy sauce + mirin 30 mins.
steps.why: Precise timing gives the perfect jammy, orange yolk.
steps.tip: The soy marinade gives eggs a beautiful amber color and savory-sweet taste.

steps.title: Cook Noodles & Assemble
steps.instruction: Cook ramen noodles separately. Place in bowl, pour hot broth. Top with mushrooms, corn, bamboo shoots, nori, egg, and scallions.
steps.why: Separate noodle cooking prevents them absorbing too much broth and going mushy.
steps.tip: Toss noodles in sesame oil before plating to prevent sticking.

steps.title: Make Teriyaki Sauce
steps.instruction: Combine soy sauce, mirin, sake, and sugar. Stir until sugar dissolves. Add minced garlic and ginger.
steps.why: The balance of sweet, salty, and umami is the essence of teriyaki.
steps.tip: Use real mirin (not mirin-style seasoning) for authentic flavor.

steps.title: Sear Chicken
steps.instruction: Pat chicken dry. Sear skin-side down in oil over medium-high heat for 5-6 mins until golden. Flip and cook 3-4 mins.
steps.why: Patting dry and searing skin-side first creates crispy skin and locks in juices.
steps.tip: Don't move the chicken — let it develop a proper sear.

steps.title: Glaze with Sauce
steps.instruction: Pour teriyaki sauce over chicken. Cook on medium heat, turning and basting every minute until sauce thickens and coats chicken (5-6 mins).
steps.why: The sauce reduces and caramelizes, creating the signature glossy teriyaki glaze.
steps.tip: Watch carefully — the sugars in the sauce can burn quickly on high heat.

steps.title: Rest & Serve
steps.instruction: Rest chicken 2-3 mins. Slice against the grain. Serve over steamed rice. Garnish with sesame seeds and scallions.
steps.why: Resting lets juices redistribute for a more tender, juicy result.
steps.tip: Drizzle any remaining pan sauce over the sliced chicken and rice.

steps.title: Cook Pasta
steps.instruction: Cook macaroni in salted boiling water until al dente (1 min less than package time). Reserve 1 cup pasta water. Drain.
steps.why: Al dente pasta won't go mushy when baked in sauce.
steps.tip: Salting pasta water generously is your only chance to season the pasta itself.

steps.title: Make Roux
steps.instruction: Melt butter in saucepan. Add flour and cook stirring for 1-2 mins until light golden.
steps.why: Cooking the flour removes the raw taste and thickens the sauce without clumping.
steps.tip: The roux should smell nutty, not raw — that's your cue it's ready.

steps.title: Build Cheese Sauce
steps.instruction: Gradually whisk in warm milk. Cook until thick. Remove from heat. Add mustard, paprika, garlic powder, then stir in cheese until melted.
steps.why: Removing from heat before adding cheese prevents a grainy sauce.
steps.tip: Grate cheese yourself — pre-shredded has anti-caking agents that prevent smooth melting.

steps.title: Combine & Top
steps.instruction: Fold in pasta. Transfer to baking dish. Top with breadcrumbs and extra cheese. Broil 3-4 mins until golden and bubbly.
steps.why: Broiling creates an irresistible golden crust with a creamy interior.
steps.tip: Watch the broiler closely — it goes from perfect to burnt in seconds!

steps.title: Season Chicken
steps.instruction: Rub chicken thighs with smoked paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, brown sugar, and salt. Let sit 15 mins.
steps.why: A dry rub penetrates the meat and creates a flavorful bark when cooked.
steps.tip: The longer you marinate, the deeper the flavor — overnight is ideal.

steps.title: Cook Low & Slow
steps.instruction: Bake seasoned chicken at 165°C (325°F) for 35-40 mins until very tender and easily shredded.
steps.why: Low slow heat breaks down collagen, making the chicken pull apart effortlessly.
steps.tip: Chicken is done when you can shred it with two forks without resistance.

steps.title: Pull & Sauce
steps.instruction: Shred chicken with two forks. Toss generously with BBQ sauce and a splash of apple cider vinegar.
steps.why: Vinegar brightens the rich BBQ sauce and keeps pulled chicken moist.
steps.tip: Warm the sauced chicken in a pan briefly to caramelize the sugars in the BBQ sauce.

steps.title: Build Sandwich
steps.instruction: Toast brioche buns. Pile high with pulled chicken. Top with creamy coleslaw and extra BBQ sauce.
steps.why: Coleslaw adds crunch and cooling creaminess to balance the smoky, saucy chicken.
steps.tip: Don't skip toasting the buns — it prevents them from getting soggy.

steps.title: Cook Pasta
steps.instruction: Cook penne in heavily salted water until al dente. Reserve 1 cup pasta water. Drain.
steps.why: Pasta water contains starch that helps sauce cling to pasta.
steps.tip: Salt pasta water generously — it should taste pleasantly salty.

steps.title: Sauté Vegetables
steps.instruction: Heat olive oil over high heat. Add garlic, then vegetables in order of hardness (asparagus first, tomatoes last). Cook 6-8 mins total.
steps.why: High heat quickly caramelizes vegetables while keeping them crisp and vibrant.
steps.tip: Add the tomatoes last — they only need 1-2 mins to blister.

steps.title: Combine
steps.instruction: Add drained pasta to vegetables. Splash in pasta water and toss vigorously over heat for 2 mins.
steps.why: Pasta water starch emulsifies with olive oil to create a light, glossy sauce.
steps.tip: Add pasta water gradually — you may not need all of it.

steps.title: Finish & Serve
steps.instruction: Remove from heat. Add lemon juice, fresh basil, and generous parmesan. Toss and serve immediately.
steps.why: Lemon brightens flavors; basil and parmesan added off heat preserve their fresh character.
steps.tip: Quality parmesan makes a huge difference — use freshly grated Parmigiano-Reggiano.

steps.title: Pound & Dredge Chicken
steps.instruction: Pound chicken breasts to even thickness (1cm). Season and dredge lightly in flour, shaking off excess.
steps.why: Even thickness ensures uniform cooking. Flour creates a golden crust and thickens the sauce.
steps.tip: Don't flour the chicken until just before cooking or it becomes gummy.

steps.title: Sear Chicken
steps.instruction: Heat olive oil and butter in a skillet over high heat. Sear chicken 3-4 mins per side until golden. Remove and set aside.
steps.why: Hard searing develops the fond (browned bits) that give the Marsala sauce its depth.
steps.tip: Don't crowd the pan — sear in batches if needed for a proper crust.

steps.title: Make Marsala Sauce
steps.instruction: In same pan sauté shallots, garlic, mushrooms. Add Marsala wine and reduce by half (3-4 mins). Add broth and cream, simmer until slightly thickened.
steps.why: Deglazing with wine picks up the fond and forms the base of the rich sauce.
steps.tip: Real Marsala wine (not 'cooking wine') makes an enormous flavor difference.

steps.title: Finish & Serve
steps.instruction: Return chicken to pan, spoon sauce over, simmer gently 5 mins. Finish with cold butter swirled in. Garnish with parsley.
steps.why: Swirling cold butter (monter au beurre) gives the sauce a silky, restaurant-quality finish.
steps.tip: Serve immediately with mashed potatoes, pasta, or crusty bread to soak up the sauce.
